sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa dell'aumento delle tensioni geopolitiche tra USA e Iran, con il Brent che sale del 7.78% e il VIX che scende del 2.73% a 23.87. Il DXY è stabile a 100.04 e l'S&P 500 registra un aumento modesto dello 0.11%.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to rising geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran, with Brent crude oil prices surging 7.78% and VIX decreasing by 2.73% to 23.87. The DXY is stable at 100.04, and the S&P 500 shows a modest increase of 0.11%.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è chiuso per la festività di Venerdì Santo. Il Brent sale del 7.78% a $109.03 a causa delle tensioni tra USA e Iran. L'oro scende del 2.75% a $4651.50/oz. Il Bitcoin registra un calo del 0.23% a $66,733.
market_impact_summary.en: Stock markets are closed for Easter Friday. Brent crude oil prices surge 7.78% to $109.03 due to US-Iran tensions. Gold decreases by 2.75% to $4651.50/oz. Bitcoin shows a decline of 0.23% to $66,733.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi, il vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano.

Mentre le borse mondiali osservano la consueta pausa festiva per la celebrazione del Venerdì Santo, analizziamo la chiusura dei mercati asiatici. Il Nikkei ha chiuso in rialzo dell'1.26% a 53,123 punti, mentre lo Shanghai ha registrato un calo dello 0.95% a 3882 punti.

Nella seduta precedente, l'S&P 500 ha mostrato una modesta crescita dello 0.11% a 6582.69 punti, con il VIX che si è attestato a 23.87 punti, in calo del 2.73%. Il Dollar Index (DXY) ha registrato una sostanziale stabilità a 100.04 punti. I tassi di interesse sono risultati invariati, con il Tasso Fed Funds stabile al 3.64%.

Sul fronte geopolitico, permane alta tensione nel conflitto tra Iran e Israele. Gli Stati Uniti hanno espresso preoccupazione per l'aumento delle ostilità e hanno invitato tutte le parti a esercitare la massima moderazione. Questa situazione continua a influenzare i mercati, in particolare il prezzo del petrolio, che ieri ha registrato un aumento significativo.

In ambito macroeconomico, gli investitori attendono con ansia i prossimi dati sul PIL degli Usa, previsto per il 29 aprile. In Europa, la BCE ha lasciato invariati i tassi di interesse al 2.00% nella riunione di ieri.

Passiamo ora al comparto digitale, che opera 24 ore su 24. Bitcoin ha registrato un calo dello 0.23% a $66,733. La capitalizzazione del mercato crypto continua a mostrare volatilità, con l'indice Fear & Greed che si attesta a 9, in territorio di Extreme Fear.

In attesa della ripresa degli scambi sui mercati tradizionali, continueremo a monitorare l'evoluzione della situazione geopolitica e i dati macroeconomici in arrivo. Restate sintonizzati per ulteriori aggiornamenti.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con un'analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.

Bitcoin è sceso leggermente, raggiungendo i $66,751, con un incremento dello 0.22%. Tuttavia, gli ETF hanno registrato un deflusso di $-173.7M, secondo i dati più recenti. Questo potrebbe indicare una certa cautela tra gli investitori istituzionali.

Ethereum è salito dello 0.77% a $2,063, mentre Solana ha guadagnato lo 0.61% a $79.54. Binance Coin, invece, ha registrato una lieve flessione del -0.40% a $587.03. Questi movimenti riflettono una certa stabilità nel mercato delle altcoin.

L'indice Fear & Greed è sceso a 9, classificandosi come Estrema Paura. Questo livello di paura potrebbe essere correlato ai recenti dati macroeconomici statunitensi, che hanno mostrato segnali contrastanti. La correlazione tra il sentiment del mercato crypto e i dati macroeconomici suggerisce che gli investitori sono ancora cauti.

In sintesi, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di attesa, con Bitcoin e altcoin che mostrano movimenti limitati. Gli investitori continuano a monitorare i dati macroeconomici e le notizie geopolitiche per prendere decisioni informate.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is risk-off today, with investors cautious amid rising tensions in the Middle East. Key data points include a decline in equities and a surge in oil prices. The VIX index is at 23.87, indicating increased volatility.

Traditional markets are closed today for the Good Friday holiday. At the last close, equities were mixed: the S&P 500 was at 6582.69, up 0.11%, while the STOXX 600 was at 59.24, down 0.20%. In the bond market, the US 10-year yield was at 4.31%, down 0.14%, and the TLT Bond USA 20Y was at 86.79, up 0.61%. The Dollar Index was at 100.04, up 0.01%, while the Euro to US Dollar exchange rate was at 1.15, down 0.41%. Commodities saw significant movements, with Brent crude at $109.03, up 7.78%, and Gold at $4651.50 per ounce, down 2.75%.

Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are escalating, impacting markets. The recent developments have led to a surge in oil prices and a flight to safe-haven assets. The macroeconomic implications of this conflict are significant, with potential effects on inflation and interest rates. The US Federal Reserve's stance on monetary policy may be influenced by these developments, particularly in light of recent economic data, including the PCE Core YoY rate of 3.1%.

While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which are open 24/7. Let's take a look at the current state of the crypto market. Bitcoin is at $66,733, down 0.23%, and the Fear & Greed Index is at 9, indicating extreme fear. Ethereum is at $2,063, up 0.77%, and Solana is at $79.54, up 0.61%. The crypto market continues to react to global events and investor sentiment.

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a closer look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

Starting with Bitcoin, we see that the BTC ETF daily net inflow has been negative at $173.7 million. From a technical standpoint, Bitcoin is trading at $66,751, up 0.22% from the previous day. The recent flows into ETFs have been quite sluggish, which could be a sign of investor caution.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the major altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,063, up 0.77%, while Solana is at $79.54, up 0.61%. Binance Coin is slightly down at $587.03, with a 0.40% decrease. These movements suggest a relatively stable but cautious market sentiment.

In terms of market sentiment and fear/greed index, we see that the index is currently at 9, indicating extreme fear. This is often a sign that investors are overly cautious or bearish, which can sometimes precede a market rebound. Historically, periods of extreme fear have been good buying opportunities for investors with a long-term perspective. However, it's essential to consider the broader market context, including macroeconomic factors and geopolitical tensions, which can significantly impact cryptocurrency markets.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A closing note from Prais Alért, see you tomorrow.

articles.title: The Iran War and the Energy Lesson We Failed to Learn
articles.snippet: How many energy crises will it take before we stop betting our economies on a fragile oil market?The U.S. and Israeli war with Iran quickly morphed from a regional conflict into a global energy shock. Oil and gas markets have been volatile since the start of the war, with Brent crude oil prices rising 59 percent since Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most important energy chokepoints. In the first half of 2025, about one-fifth of the world’s oil and liquified natural gas passed through the strait. Now, five weeks into the conflict, attacks The post The Iran War and the Energy Lesson We Failed to Learn appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.summary_en: The escalating tensions between the US and Iran have had a significant impact on the energy market.

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: Climate change and central banking: macroeconomic challenges and evidence from Africa
articles.snippet: Climate change is an important source of macroeconomic risk with direct implications for monetary policy and financial stability. Physical risks, including more frequent and severe weather events, disrupt production and damage infrastructure, generating supply and demand side pressures that affect output and inflation.

articles.title: From Filament to Firepower: 3D Printing’s Impact on Warfare
articles.snippet: While much ink has been spilled over how 3D printing has enabled intense drone-on-drone warfare in Ukraine, the U.S. defense and intelligence communities have overlooked a stealthier development: Additive manufacturing is revolutionizing how guns are produced, fielded, and sustained in armed conflicts, especially by non-state actors. What once required a web of smuggling networks, foreign sponsors, and captured stockpiles can now be made with digital files and off-the-shelf parts. Even ammunition production, once considered an insurmountable barrier, is increasingly possible. This revolution in how arms and ammunition are manufactured has yielded resilient and decentralized supply chains that can endure both The post From Filament to Firepower: 3D Printing’s Impact on Warfare appeared firs

articles.title: Why US-China Decoupling Isn’t Happening
articles.snippet: Despite ongoing debates about Sino-American decoupling, the global economy remains as integrated as ever. Whenever either superpower tries to restrict ties with the other, it accelerates a broader process of adaptation and adjustment that is making the system more resilient and harder to control unilaterally.

articles.title: Fuel Shock Forces Airlines Into Emergency Mode
articles.snippet: Soaring jet fuel prices have hit the profitability of airlines, who have started raising air fares and grounding flights to contain the fallout from the Iran war, which has more than doubled aviation fuel prices over the past month. Oil and jet fuel supplies are constrained as crude and petroleum products are trapped at the Strait of Hormuz, forcing Asian refiners to cut run rates and Asian countries to restrict or ban exports to preserve domestic supply. Jet Fuel Shock The product market came under more severe stress than the crude markets as…

articles.title: Donbas Coal Deal Exposes Fragile Enforcement of Russia Sanctions
articles.snippet: A Georgian company has struck a deal to import coal, metals, and chemical products from the Russian-occupied Ukrainian region of Donetsk, a move that raises fresh questions about sanctions enforcement and underscores Tbilisi's tightening economic ties with Moscow. The agreement involves George Oil Ltd, owned by Valerian Kochiashvili, who has openly expressed his intention to trade with Russia and use raw materials from Ukraine’s Donbas to access markets beyond EU restrictions. The entrepreneur confirmed the arrangement to Georgian media,…

articles.title: Oil Majors Eye U.S. Offshore Asset as Supply Crunch Intensifies
articles.snippet: TotalEnergies and Shell are among the companies interested in buying a majority stake in the Shenandoah oil and gas field that is being sold by Beacon Offshore Energy and HEQ Deepwater. Per a Reuters report citing unnamed sources, other interested parties in the 51% stake include BP, Repsol, and Chevron. The heightened interest in the offshore field comes amid the unprecedented supply disruption in Middle East oil and gas, amid the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran. Beacon Offshore Energy is financially backed by Blackstone, while HEQ Deepwater is owned…

articles.title: $100 Oil Isn’t Enough to Balance Alberta’s Books
articles.snippet: Oil markets reversed their recent downtrend on Thursday, with oil prices surging after President Donald Trump declared that the U.S. would continue to hit Iran "extremely hard" for the next two to three weeks. Trump warned that he would hit all of Iran's electric generating plants if a deal is not reached, sending the country back to the Stone Age. Trump’s bellicosity marks a sudden shift in policy, suggesting that securing the Strait of Hormuz is no longer Washington’s top priority. Brent crude for May delivery was up 7.58% to trade…

articles.title: 40% of Russia's Baltic Oil Export Capacity Offline as Gasoline Ban Widens
articles.snippet: Russia is banning gasoline producers from exporting the fuel until July 31, expanding the current ban that applied only to non-producers, as the Middle East war and the Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian export terminals have upended the global and domestic markets. The Russian government on Thursday said it had expanded the ban on gasoline exports to producers of the fuel, Russian news agency Interfax reported. Russia has had a ban on gasoline exports by non-producers, but now the measure is expanding to gasoline producers. The new ban exempts…

articles.title_en: Bitcoin miner Soluna acquires $53M wind farm to power AI facility
articles.summary_it: Soluna expanded into artificial intelligence in 2024 to shore up declining revenues from its crypto mining business, mirroring an industry-wide shift toward AI workloads.
articles.summary_en: Soluna expanded into artificial intelligence in 2024 to shore up declining revenues from its crypto mining business, mirroring an industry-wide shift toward AI workloads.

articles.title: Bitcoin hits weekly low on oil fears as analyst teases $10K BTC price target
articles.snippet: Analysis warned that Bitcoin risked falling to $10,000 in the long term as BTC price action fell with US stocks amid oil-supply concerns.
articles.title_it: Bitcoin hits weekly low on oil fears as analyst teases $10K BTC price target
articles.title_en: Bitcoin hits weekly low on oil fears as analyst teases $10K BTC price target
